messages.content: 用简洁的英文对这篇报告做总结

This report evaluates K-Means clustering as an unsupervised baseline for semantic segmentation on the COCO-Stuff dataset (182 classes). It highlights K-Means’ computational efficiency but shows that its segmentation quality is fundamentally limited because it relies only on RGB color similarity rather than semantic understanding. Key failure modes include lack of semantic context (confusing different objects with similar colors), over-segmentation due to texture/color variation within the same object, and sensitivity to lighting and shadows. Overall, the work positions K-Means as a useful “performance floor” baseline when no semantic learning is involved, underscoring the difficulty of COCO-Stuff. [1]
